steps.title: Identify the relative clause type
steps.content: "One European city" calls for a restrictive clause that identifies which city — the one many travelers recommend.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive clause rule
steps.content:
"One European city" needs a restrictive clause to identify which city — the one many travelers recommend. Restrictive clauses use "that" with no comma.
  • Choice A — "city, that": "one European city, that many travelers recommend" — a comma before "that" in a restrictive clause is non-standard; restrictive "that" never takes a preceding comma. ✗
  • Choice B — "city, which": "one European city, which many travelers recommend" — "which" with a comma signals a non-restrictive clause (adds extra info); here the clause is identifying, not extra. ✗
  • Choice C — "city in which,": "one European city in which, many travelers recommend" — "recommend in which" is ungrammatical; you recommend a city, not recommend in a city. ✗
  • Choice D — "city that": "one European city that many travelers recommend" — restrictive "that," no comma; identifies the city correctly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
